steps.title: Set up the equation using slope-intercept form
steps.content:
We're given the slope $m = -6$ and y-intercept at $(0, 48)$. Using the slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$, the equation of line $k$ is:
$$y = -6x + 48$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept by setting y equals 0
steps.content:
The x-intercept occurs where the line crosses the x-axis, which means $y = 0$. Substitute into the equation:
$$0 = -6x + 48$$
Solve for $x$:
$$6x = 48$$
$$x = 8$$
The x-coordinate of the x-intercept is 8.
